must_have_breakdown.evidence: Resume lists 'Strong expertise in AUTOSAR' under key skills but provides no evidence of development work — only testing context.
2 years on Skoda ADAS project testing Forward Collision Warning, Parking Assistance, MVC, RVC, ACC, and Ultrasonic sensors using HIL bench and DYNA4.

reasoning: Reject — wrong role. This candidate is a System Test Engineer, not a Software Developer. The JD is looking for a Senior SW Developer with hands-on C programming, MISRA coding standards, AUTOSAR stack development, and RTOS expertise — none of which this candidate demonstrates. Their AUTOSAR mention is surface-level and testing-only; they have zero evidence of embedded C development, MISRA compliance, or RTOS work. The only meaningful overlap is ADAS domain exposure from 2 years of system-level testing on the Skoda project, but that is testing, not development. RADAR knowledge — a key differentiator for this JD — is entirely absent. Even if the pool is thin, this candidate would need a complete career pivot to be relevant. Do not shortlist.
strengths: ADAS DOMAIN EXPOSURE: Two years of hands-on ADAS system testing on Skoda's Forward Collision Avoidance and parking systems, including HIL bench simulation with DYNA4 and VT-Studio — provides relevant domain context even though the work is test-side.

AUTOMOTIVE PROTOCOL DEPTH: Proficient across CAN, CANFD, CAN-TP, UDS, and Automotive Ethernet with working knowledge of diagnostic service IDs and DTC analysis — signals solid ECU communication fundamentals.

AUTOSAR AWARENESS: Claims AUTOSAR familiarity and has worked in ASPICE and ISO 26262 compliant environments, which shows exposure to the automotive development framework even if not from a development role.
gaps: NO EMBEDDED C OR MISRA EXPERIENCE: The JD's primary requirement is strong C programming with MISRA compliance — this candidate lists no C development, no MISRA awareness, and no software development experience of any kind. This is a hard disqualifier.

NO RTOS OR RADAR KNOWLEDGE: RTOS is listed as mandatory in the JD; RADAR system knowledge and algorithm development are key differentiators. The resume shows zero evidence of either — this candidate operates entirely on the test side of the V-model.

scoring_factors.reason: No notice period stated in resume; no JD maximum specified — no penalty applied.
Candidate is based in Chennai (Jasmin Infotech, Chennai 600100) matching the JD's in-office Chennai requirement.
Candidate has 4 years of experience meeting the JD's minimum of 4 years relevant experience — no penalty.
JD gender preference is 'Any' — no penalty applied.
No college preference or exclusion specified in the JD — no adjustment applied.

availability_reason: Current role at Jasmin Infotech is listed as 'March 2022 to present' with no notice period stated.
rare_assets: HIL bench experience with DYNA4 and VT-Studio for ADAS closed-loop simulation — niche test capability in automotive validation.
self_reported_unverified: 'Strong expertise in AUTOSAR' — listed as a skill but no development-level AUTOSAR work or specific layer/module involvement is described anywhere in the resume.
'Significantly reducing manual testing time and improving efficiency' via CAPL scripting — no metric or quantified outcome provided to support this claim.
ISO 26262 functional safety compliance — mentioned as a standard adhered to but no evidence of active participation in a safety case, FMEA, or safety analysis activity.
interview_flags: Probe the actual depth of AUTOSAR knowledge — can they name specific layers (RTE, BSW, MCAL) and have they ever configured or developed within the AUTOSAR stack, or only tested against it?
Ask directly: have they written any embedded C code outside of CAPL scripting? CAPL is not embedded C.
Clarify the MISRA claim — do they have any awareness of MISRA-C guidelines even as a tester reviewing code?
Probe RTOS exposure — have they ever worked with FreeRTOS, OSEK, or any real-time OS in any capacity?
Verify RADAR system exposure — the Skoda project mentions ultrasonic and camera sensors; probe whether they have any radar-specific (77GHz/24GHz) validation or algorithm testing experience.
